focus: A1 launcher/IPC concurrency: owner-lease reclaim, EPERM classification, socket-server copy drift, cross-launcher parity

findingDetails.title: EPERM owner-lease is permanently un-reclaimable; heartbeat-staleness gate is structurally unreachable for EPERM owners
findingDetails.evidence: classifyOwnerLease returns 'unknown-eperm' (line 339) before the heartbeat-staleness gate (346-348); acquireOwnerLeaseFile (361) buckets unknown-eperm with live-owner -> acquired:false with no staleness fall-through. EPERM is reachable in sandboxed runtimes (code comment line 480). Introduced in-range by 3419e0a3e9.
findingDetails.recommendation: Let EPERM leases still age out via the 2xTTL heartbeat gate (or an EPERM-specific longer TTL) instead of treating them as immortal.
findingDetails.scopeProof: in-range (git log -S stale-heartbeat-reclaim -> 3419e0a3e9); EPERM return + heartbeat gate both in classifyOwnerLease, consumed at line-361 branch
findingDetails.claim: An EPERM owner lease is classified live-owner-equivalent and can never be reclaimed via heartbeat staleness, wedging the launcher until manual lease deletion.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Searched acquireOwnerLeaseFile + classifyOwnerLease + reapOwnerBeforeRespawn for any EPERM-aging escape; none exists.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: EPERM owners intended immortal (genuinely live but unreadable); rejected because PID-reuse into a foreign-uid process also yields EPERM and the heartbeat gate exists to cover that but is unreachable.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: If deployment guarantees same-uid owner (EPERM impossible), drop to P2.

findingDetails.title: code-graph keeps a full unsynced fork of the shared socket-server whose contract says 'cannot drift'; no shim/import/guard
findingDetails.evidence: spec-kit + advisor are 22-line re-export shims (export ... from '@spec-kit/shared/ipc/socket-server.js'); spec-kit shim header states the security contract 'cannot drift between services'. code-graph holds a full 402-line copy (diff -u exit 0 today) whose header falsely claims to be 'canonical'. No sync/drift guard found under .opencode/skills/*/scripts. Future shared fixes will not propagate to code-graph.
findingDetails.recommendation: Make code-graph a re-export shim like the other two, OR add a CI drift guard asserting byte-equality vs shared.
findingDetails.scopeProof: all four socket-server files in git diff --name-only a9e9bdb0a5^..HEAD; diff -u shared vs code-graph exit 0; two shims re-export @spec-kit/shared, code-graph does not
affectedSurfaceHints: code-graph socket-server should be a shim
code-graph header mislabels a fork as canonical
findingDetails.claim: code-graph's socket-server is a full unsynced fork of the shared canonical, contradicting the documented cannot-drift invariant.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Grepped .opencode/skills/*/scripts for socket-server sync/drift guard; none found.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: code-graph intentionally vendors its own copy; rejected as clean because no drift guard + cannot-drift contract makes silent divergence inevitable and the canonical header claim is false.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: If a build/test already asserts code-graph==shared byte-equality (not found), drop to P2 maintainability.

findingDetails.title: Reclaim-path owner-lease write skips fsync unlike the exclusive fresh-acquire writer; durability asymmetry
findingDetails.evidence: Fresh-acquire CAS-writes with openSync(wx)+fsyncSync (durable). Reclaim path and heartbeat refresh use writeOwnerLeaseFile: tmp + renameSync with no file/parent-dir fsync. Crash after rename can lose the reclaimed lease, reopening the double-owner window. Re-read (383-384) serializes in-memory winner so not an active bug.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add fsyncSync (file + parent dir) to writeOwnerLeaseFile rename path for parity, or document weaker reclaim durability.
findingDetails.scopeProof: both writers in-range (3419e0a3e9); fsync present line 294, absent 281-285
affectedSurfaceHints: writeOwnerLeaseFile fsync parity with exclusive writer

findingDetails.title: processLiveness duplicated verbatim in mk-code-index-launcher instead of importing the shared model-server-supervision copy; drift risk
findingDetails.evidence: spec-memory imports processLiveness from shared lib (mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:35); code-index defines a byte-identical local copy. Currently in sync; the ESRCH/EPERM liveness contract drives lease reclaim (see F-002) and can now diverge with no guard.
findingDetails.recommendation: code-index should require('./lib/model-server-supervision.cjs').processLiveness like spec-memory, or add a parity guard.
findingDetails.scopeProof: code-index launcher in-range; both bodies read and confirmed identical; spec-memory imports shared at line 35

noveltyJustification: All 4 findings are net-new (registry openFindings was empty). Weighted new = 2*P1(5)+2*P2(1)=12; weighted total=12; ratio=1.0. No prior-finding refinement this pass.
traceabilityChecks: range integrity HEAD 12de3d3a7e base f05bdac2cf re-confirmed
F-002 classification ordering added by in-range 3419e0a3e9 (git log -S)
edgeCases: reclaim TOCTOU resolved: non-exclusive write + re-read (383-384) serializes to one owner, residual is durability only (F-004)
PID reuse bounded by 2xTTL heartbeat EXCEPT EPERM case (F-002 unreachable gate)
charter partially refuted: code-index HAS owner-lease but lacks sessionProxy/JSON-RPC-error surface
ruledOut: lease-CAS reclaim TOCTOU as P0 (re-read serializes; residual demoted to P2 F-004 via Hunter/Skeptic/Referee)
LEASE_HELD_BY stdout colliding with proxied client stdio (session proxy owns stdout; raw onError marker intentionally unused, lines 195-201; LEASE_HELD_BY only on report/no-bridge paths)
confirmedClean: owner socket-path staleness (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:344-353): stored path used only if fs.existsSync, else falls back to recompute then no-bridge-socket+deep-probe; no stale-socket bridge defect

integrationEvidence.fact: owner-lease tokens=0 (confirms charter: advisor has NO owner-lease)
owner-lease tokens=9 (refutes charter 'code-index missing owner-lease'); sessionProxy=0 (lacks recycle-surviving session proxy)
spec-kit+advisor=22-line shims re-exporting @spec-kit/shared; code-graph=402-line fork; diff -u shared vs code-graph exit 0

nextFocus.target: memory-save.ts partial-write/crash safety + transaction rollback (~2860-2897), enrichment-state.ts, default-on enrichment blast radius, post-insert exec-status collapse (~435-499), E081->E085-089 (response-builder ~495-533), entity-density cache invalidation (mutation-hooks)
